title: Design of I2C Protocol in Verilog-A New Approach
author: A, Smitha
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Serial communication is a technique where the transfer of data happens serially bitwise. Parallel communication is a technique where the transfer of data happens parallelly simultaneously bitwise. In many applications serial communication is preferred. Comparison between I2C, SPI and CAN serial communication protocol can be seen. I2C communication protocol is preferred over SPI and CAN protocols. I2C is a synchronous serial communication which can be implemented in Verilog.
